events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer exactly one valid JSON object no prose. Need assess scenario: commercial electrical terminations & splices, rough-complete, insufficient torque (thermal signature IR). Reference NEC 110.14. Need identify component/condition and visible defect cues. decision fail because non-compliant/dangerous. risk high? Insufficient torque causes overheating, fire, critical maybe. Let's decide. component_conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete maybe 90. value could be temperature? Not given. Could leave 0. confidence 0.95. event_time 0, rate 0, order empty, workable empty, sound_source empty. findings: loose terminations/splices, inadequate torque, elevated resistance causing overheating per IR. actions: torque to manufacturer spec with calibrated torque screwdriver/wrench, reterminate, retest IR. references: NEC 110.14. Need produce raw JSON. Ensure fields match schema.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": [
    "installed-defective",
    "non-compliant"
  ],
  "percent_complete": 90,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Electrical terminations/splices installed with insufficient torque",
    "Thermal anomaly detected by infrared inspection indicating elevated resistance and overheating",
    "Non-compliant with NEC 110.14 requirements for proper terminal tightening"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "De-energize circuit and verify torque of all terminations and splices per manufacturer specifications",
    "Reterminate or re-splice affected connections using calibrated torque tools",
    "Re-inspect with infrared thermography and document post-repair temperatures",
    "Verify conductor preparation and termination compatibility before re-energizing"
  ],
  "rationale": "Insufficient torque on electrical terminations and splices increases contact resistance, producing heat that appears as a thermal signature on IR inspection. NEC 110.14 requires terminals and splices to be tightened to manufacturer specifications; failure to do so creates a fire and equipment failure hazard in rough-complete commercial electrical work.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 110.14"
  ]
}
